task: Replace manual httpx-based OAuth 2.0 PKCE implementation with google-auth-oauthlib Flow API, and remove the unused IndexService.create_google_doc_datasource dead-code method.

key_reasoning.reason: Security-critical OAuth/PKCE code path being replaced; async/sync transport mismatch between httpx.AsyncClient (current) and google.auth.transport.requests.Request (library default, synchronous) risks event-loop blocking; no dedicated test file exists for GoogleOAuthSettingsService; behavioral differences between the manual PKCE implementation and Flow (state handling, error shapes) must be explicitly validated. Auth red flag bumped this from L (4) to XL (5).
Three components touched: google_oauth_settings_service.py (primary, 536 lines), google_oauth.py router, and index_service.py (dead code deletion). Spans Service and API layers.

red_flags_applied: Technical Risk bumped from L (4) to XL (5): affects authentication and authorization (OAuth 2.0 PKCE token exchange and refresh flow is security-critical)
Borderline score 20 (top of M range) leaned higher to L because Technical Risk is XL (5) per guide borderline rule
